steps.title: Find when the coefficient equals f(1)
steps.content: The coefficient equals $f(1)$ only when the exponential part becomes $1$ at $x=1$. That happens when the exponent is $0$ at $x=1$.

steps.title: Evaluate f(1) for Choice C — $f \left(x\right)=128{ \left(2\right)}^{x-1}$
steps.content:
For Choice C — $f \left(x\right)=128{ \left(2\right)}^{x-1}$, $f(x)=128(2)^{x-1}$:
$$f(1)=128(2)^{1-1}=128(2)^0=128$$
The coefficient is $k$, so Choice C — $f \left(x\right)=128{ \left(2\right)}^{x-1}$.
